text:
​@Isaiah-h8n "Small webs! Big webs! Spider-man webs are catchy webs! Webs around fingers! Webs around legs!"

Sentence-mixer brain will immediately lock on to words like "big" /bɪɡ/ because you can do so much with it; in this case take the first part "bi-" /bɪ/ and you're already halfway there, sometimes we have to approximate the "-tch" sound with a "sh" but in this case you have exactly what you need just a few words later with "catchy" /ˈkæt͡ʃi/ because it has the exact /t͡ʃ/ form used in "bitch" /bɪt͡ʃ/
